payload.summary: The weekly review pulse indicates that the application has several issues with usability and navigation, functional issues and bugs, and support and transparency. Many users are facing problems with the app's interface, cashback usage, and customer support. Despite some positive reviews, the overall sentiment is negative, with users experiencing frustration and disappointment with the app's performance.
quotes: The app is too difficult to navigate, it's like a maze. I started SIP, money deducted but not showing in SIP section, I'm unable to find where is SIP.

I have been using INDmoney for the last 5 months and earned ₹225 cashback through regular UPI transactions. But now it is restricted to stock purchases only, making my earned cashback practically useless.

The support team is not the best... app glitches at times saying 'something went wrong' while paying to the concerned bank to change to dollars... wish I had other options rather than creating bank accounts
action_items: Improve the app's navigation and user interface to make it more user-friendly and easy to use.
Address the issues with cashback usage and provide more flexible options for users to utilize their rewards.
Enhance customer support by providing timely and effective assistance to users, and ensuring that their concerns are resolved promptly.
action_ideas: Improve the app's navigation and user interface to make it more user-friendly and easy to use.
Address the issues with cashback usage and provide more flexible options for users to utilize their rewards.
Enhance customer support by providing timely and effective assistance to users, and ensuring that their concerns are resolved promptly.

explanation_bullets: A mutual fund exit load is a fee charged to investors when they redeem or withdraw their units from a mutual fund scheme.
The exit load is deducted from the redemption proceeds and is expressed as a percentage of the withdrawal amount.
The purpose of an exit load is to discourage investors from withdrawing their money too quickly, thereby helping the fund manager to maintain a stable portfolio.
The exit load percentage and the period for which it is applicable vary across mutual fund schemes.
Some mutual fund schemes may have a nil exit load or a lower exit load if the units are held for a certain period.
The exit load structure is clearly disclosed in the mutual fund scheme's offer document and on the website of the mutual fund house.
A brokerage fee is a charge levied by a broker or brokerage firm for facilitating the buying and selling of securities.
This fee can be a flat rate or a percentage of the transaction amount.
Brokerage fees are typically paid by the investor and can vary depending on the type of investment and the brokerage firm.
The fee is usually deducted from the investor's account at the time of the transaction.
Brokerage fees can apply to a wide range of investments, including stocks, bonds, and mutual funds.
The amount of the brokerage fee can impact the overall cost of the investment and the investor's returns.

Though taxation and ITR filing rules are quite complicated even when investing a small amount in foreign markets, INDmoney has done a commendable job of minimizing these complexities. The platform provides accurate, well-structured reports, clearly identifying the relevant periods, amounts and the appropriate sections to be used while filing ITR. It is a very good platform. The financial learning courses they have recently introduced are also highly appreciated.

US Stock investment Deposit conversion rate is locked instantly, but during withdrawal the USD to INR conversion happens later and the final exchange rate is informed afterwards. Withdrawal processing also takes a long time. Due to delayed conversion, forex spread and extra charges, even after earning profit in USD I received less INR than my original investment. The withdrawal and conversion process should be more transparent and rates should be locked at the time of withdrawal request.
